name: Sun Bin
description: Sun Bin, a brilliant military strategist of the Warring States period in Chinese history (c. 4th century BCE), is renowned for his strategic genius and tactical innovations despite facing immense personal tragedy. Originally a student alongside Pang Juan under the tutelage of the recluse Guiguzi, Sun Bin surpassed Pang Juan in skill, igniting Pang Juan's jealousy. Pang Juan, serving in the state of Wei, tricked Sun Bin into joining him, then falsely accused him of treason and had him brutally crippled, his kneecaps removed, leaving him permanently disabled. Feigning madness to survive, Sun Bin eventually escaped to the state of Qi. There, he was discovered by the Qi general Tian Ji, who recognized his extraordinary intellect. Sun Bin became Tian Ji's strategist, devising plans that secured victories against Wei, including the famous Battle of Guiling, where he exploited Wei's overconfidence and used a 'besiege Wei to rescue Zhao' tactic. His most celebrated triumph was at the Battle of Maling, where he lured Pang Juan and the Wei army into an ambush, resulting in Pang Juan's death and a decisive victory for Qi. Sun Bin's military theories and strategies are preserved in the 'Sun Bin's Art of War,' a foundational text of Chinese military thought, emphasizing deception, exploiting enemy weaknesses, and adapting to changing circumstances. His life exemplifies resilience, strategic brilliance, and the triumph of intellect over adversity. He is considered a pivotal figure in Chinese military history, whose teachings continue to influence modern strategic thinking.
subtitle: Crippled strategist, unmatched intellect.
achievements: Victory at the Battle of Guiling
Victory at the Battle of Maling
Developed the 'besiege Wei to rescue Zhao' tactic

keyMyths.title: The Crippling of Sun Bin
keyMyths.description: Pang Juan, jealous of Sun Bin's superior intellect, falsely accused him of treason and had his kneecaps removed, rendering him crippled. This act of betrayal fueled Sun Bin's desire for revenge and shaped his strategic thinking.

keyMyths.title: Besiege Wei to Rescue Zhao
keyMyths.description: During the Battle of Guiling, Sun Bin advised Tian Ji to attack the Wei capital instead of directly engaging the Wei army besieging Zhao. This forced Wei to withdraw and saved Zhao, showcasing Sun Bin's strategic brilliance.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Maling Ambush
keyMyths.description: Sun Bin lured Pang Juan's army into a narrow gorge at Maling, setting up a devastating ambush. He marked a tree with the words 'Pang Juan dies here' and waited for Pang Juan to arrive, resulting in Pang Juan's death and a major victory for Qi.

sources.author: Sima Qian
sources.description: Provides a biographical account of Sun Bin and his exploits.
sources.text: Provides a biographical account of Sun Bin and his exploits.
